place_results.title: CVS
place_results.description: Drugstore chain selling a variety of beauty & health products, plus some grocery & household items.

places.title: MoneyGram
places.address: Inside Cvs, 222 E 34th St, New York, NY 10016

summary.snippet: "Staff is always friendly and they have 4 self checkout registers."
"The employees at this location are total garbage- so unhelpful and lazy."
"Seems to be staffed by people who have no idea where things are"

most_relevant.description: If I could give it less than 1 star I would. Its flu season, im here at 1:20pm with an infant and my son looking for my prescription. They close the gates and basically said too bad we need to take lunch. I understand and respect that, but the lack of compassion from the staff was incredible. She had the time to purchase a bloom drink infront of me but not to help get me home. Mind you its snow weather outside as well. There are better ways to handle things. This wasnt it.

By far the worst pharmacy ever.
Seeing as I could not get through (again) to the pharmacy by phone - mind you after THEY call me back and then leave me on hold for over 10m, I went over to follow up on my prescription.
After clearly explaining my situation (and also pointing out that I understand back of house is busy), I’m met by the pharmacist who did nothing but gaslight me and evade my very clear question.

Really terrible experience.  To my understanding, she also administer’s vaccines.  I will definitely not trust her to administer any to me if she can’t even answer clear and basic questions.

Had my doctor fill a prescription and received a voicemail 3 weeks ago that it would be filled that Friday or Monday. 2 weeks later, I’ve called multiple times with no update on order and no assistance, just being told to “wait until it comes in”. While 2 of the pharmacists explained the situation with no update, Linda was very rude and abrupt about it.

TLDR: 3 weeks later and still no meds or help. Switching to another pharmacy.

They worked hard to fill a prescription that I needed badly as I am traveling and in a lot of discomfort. They were so awesome, helping me out a lot even though it was closing time. I wish I'd caught the name of the guy who helped but the team worked together. Love it.

My name Santos, I just left the pharmacy! If you considering doing business with this location Be prepared to be disappointed service wise and dismissed abruptly. Especially by the crooked eyed glasses wearing white Asian man that can’t give any direction as far as handling the situation . It was I can’t help you next. I have never experienced that kind of service in my whole life.Then when I asked to speak to a manager nobody wanted to tell me. They were being vague the audacit!  the manager or head pharmacist Middle Eastern with hazel eyes terrible terrible experience run the cameras back. I arrive there at 4:50 PM had long hair in a pony tail and a Black Crossbody June 12,2025.  I would warn you not to because both the pharmacist and the pharmacist lead were unprofessional. I promise you run the tape back and start asking coworkers exactly how it went down. It was disturbing. Y’all really need to issue some type of customer service training asap to the entire staff. furthermore, some kind of documentation or probationary period needs to happen because the way I seen people treated after me is wild. Management needs to be sharply questioned and whats Service looking like moving forward! somebody’s not doing their job as far as this CVS Operation. Your better going to the Duane Reid or target up the block. I will attach a photo of one of the employees and the front of the store so there’s no confusion

As we know some items are locked behind shelves requiring a key, and it is frustrating that when I pressed the help button, no one came to assist me. Despite multiple employees (about 3 or 4) being on the same shift, not a single one made an effort to help.
I was only able to get the name of one employee “ Joy  “ but the problem seems to reflect the attitude of the entire team working at that time.
This store shows a complete lack of customer service, and I strongly disagree with the way they treat customers. Extremely disappointed and will avoid this location in the future.

Sorry for the bad review. Let me be more specific:
I entered CVS to get a flu shot.
Ask 2 employees which line I should wait in. The second gentleman pointed at the pharmacy pickup line. I waited there patiently. Once it was my turn, the gentleman directed me to the drop-off. I went to the drop-off line and waited there patiently. Once it was my turn, the lady was on her phone and dismissive of me. Once I ask, without paying attention to me, she directed me to the consultation line. I waited in the consultation line patiently. Once it was my turn she gave me forms and sent me back to pickup. I waited patiently in line. Once it was my turn, the gentleman took my forms back to the consultation and then he asked me to wait for 15 minutes. After 15mins I had to wave my hand to remind them about it.

I think it was unnecessary for patients to have to wait in 4 different lines. But maybe normal.

The vaccine administration was the best part.

Do NOT come here if you have any type of ADHD medication. I understand that the staff are overworked and that there is a shortage of ADHD meds, but it seems like this place in particular doesn’t like to fill ADHD meds for some reason (other people have had this problem too). The pharmacist was extremely rude and wouldn’t help locate if any stores nearby had my medication in stock. Please hire some nicer staff, but overall, ADHD people need to stay away from this location.
